details.Architecture_Style: Sikh Architecture (Fusion of Islamic and Hindu styles)
details.Historical_Significance: The holiest site of Sikhism, it symbolizes human brotherhood and equality. It is famous for its 24-carat gold covering and the sacred Amrit Sarovar.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Continuous kirtan (devotional singing) and Langar (community kitchen).
details.Transport_Info: Well-connected by Amritsar Junction and Sri Guru Ram Dass Jee International Airport.

details.Historical_Significance: A sacred cave at 16,000 feet where a natural ice lingam forms annually.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Annual Amarnath Yatra pilgrimage.
details.Transport_Info: Base camps at Pahalgam or Baltal; requires a high-altitude trek.

details.Historical_Significance: One of the 12 Jyotirlingas, located on the western bank of the Ganges.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Sapta Rishi Aarti and Ganga Snan.
details.Transport_Info: Connected via Varanasi Junction and Lal Bahadur Shastri Airport.

details.Historical_Significance: Part of the Char Dham yatra, believed to have been built by the Pandavas and revived by Adi Shankara.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Darshan of the conical rock formation.
details.Transport_Info: Accessible by a 16 km trek from Gaurikund.

details.Historical_Significance: Renowned for its 14 towering gopurams and the 'Hall of Thousand Pillars'. It is the spiritual heart of Madurai.
details.Managing_Authority: Hindu Religious and Charitable Endowments (HR&CE) Dept.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Ritual bath in Potramarai Kulam (Golden Lotus tank).
details.Transport_Info: 2 km from Madurai Junction railway station.

details.Historical_Significance: One of the most popular and richest temples in India. The idol is carved from a single black stone with the trunk tilted to the right.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Tuesday special Aartis and Sankashti Chaturthi pujas.
details.Transport_Info: Located at Prabhadevi, easily accessible via Dadar station.

details.Historical_Significance: Surrounded by 18 hills in the Periyar Tiger Reserve. One of the world's largest annual pilgrimage sites.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Pilgrims observe 41 days of penance before climbing the 18 sacred steps.
details.Transport_Info: 93 km from Chengannur railhead; requires 5 km trek from Pamba.

details.Historical_Significance: Part of the Char Dham, famous for its annual Rath Yatra.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Daily Mahaprasad distribution.
details.Transport_Info: Terminal railway station at Puri; 60 km from Bhubaneswar Airport.

details.Historical_Significance: Built by Gopalrao Butti as the final resting place (Samadhi) of Sai Baba. It is a symbol of inter-religious harmony.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Kakad Aarti, Madhyan Aarti, and Shej Aarti.
details.Transport_Info: Connected by Sainagar Shirdi station and Shirdi Airport.

details.Historical_Significance: Riches pilgrimage center in the world. Located atop Seshachalam Hills (Seven Hills).
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Suprabhatam and Laddu distribution as Prasadam.
details.Transport_Info: 22 km from Tirupati railway station; accessible by well-laid mountain road.

details.Historical_Significance: Important historical significance of Ayyappa Temple #1. [1]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bhajan, Archana [2]
details.Transport_Info: Bus, Train, Airport [2]

details.Historical_Significance: It is the main temple of Bhubaneswar (Ekamra Kshetra), boasting a massive spire. It represents a beautiful synthesis of Shaivism and Vaishnavism [3-6].
details.Managing_Authority: Archaeological Survey of India & Temple Trust
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing in the nearby Bindu Sagar lake before partaking in the darshan of Lord Lingaraj [8].
details.Transport_Info: Located around 18 miles from Cuttack, accessible easily from Bhubaneswar railway station on the Howrah-Waltair line [6].

details.Historical_Significance: Situated on the Nilachal Hill, it is one of the supreme 51 Shakti Peethas where Goddess Sati's reproductive organ (Yoni) fell. [9, 10]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Tantric worship rituals and bathing in the Brahmaputra river. [9, 10]
details.Transport_Info: Accessible via Amingaon railway station and Guwahati proper, crossing the Brahmaputra by steamer or bridge. [10]

details.Historical_Significance: One of the 12 sacred Jyotirlingas, situated near the banks of the holy Shipra river. Uniquely, the Jyotirlinga here faces south (Dakshinamurti) and sits below ground level. [12-14]
details.Festivals_Celebrated: Maha Shivaratri, Kumbh Mela (Simhastha every 12 years) [15, 16]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bhasma Aarti, where the deity is worshipped with fresh ashes every morning. [17]
details.Transport_Info: Ujjain is well-connected by major railway lines from Bhopal, Nagda, and Indore. [16, 17]

details.Historical_Significance: Located on the Mandhata island in the Narmada river, shaped like the sacred symbol 'Om'. It houses one of the 12 Jyotirlingas. [19-21]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Parikrama (circumambulation) of the Mandhata island and taking a holy dip in the Narmada river. [25, 26]
details.Transport_Info: Omkareshwar Road station is on the Ajmer-Khandwa line; from there, it is a 7-mile journey to the Narmada riverbank. [27, 28]

details.Build_Date: Rebuilt in 18th Century by Ahilyabai Holkar [29]
details.Historical_Significance: Renowned for a 40-centimeter-long footprint of Lord Vishnu stamped into solid rock, where he subdued the demon Gayasur. It is the premier site in India for ancestral offerings. [29-31]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Performing Pind Daan and Shraddha for ancestors, and bathing in the Falgu river. [29, 31]
details.Transport_Info: Gaya is a major junction on the Eastern Railway's main line. [33]

details.Historical_Significance: A rare site that is simultaneously one of the 12 Jyotirlingas and one of the 51 Shakti Peethas (where Sati's neck fell). Adi Shankaracharya and Shivaji Maharaj meditated here. [34, 35, 37]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing in the Patal Ganga (Krishna river) deep in the valley before worship. [39]
details.Transport_Info: The nearest major railway station is Kurnool; accessible via a steep forest motor-bus route. [39, 40]

details.Historical_Significance: Established by Lord Rama himself before bridging the sea to Lanka. It is a Jyotirlinga and features some of the longest and most spectacular temple corridors in the world. [41, 43, 45]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Pilgrims ritually bathe in the 22 sacred Tirthas (wells) inside the temple grounds and the Agni Tirtha (sea) outside. [48, 49]
details.Transport_Info: Connected to the mainland by the famous Pamban Bridge on the Southern Railway line from Madras. [50, 51]

details.Historical_Significance: Situated on the holy Venkatadri hill, it is one of the most visited and wealthiest pilgrimage centers globally. Lord Vishnu resides here to save mankind in Kali Yuga. [57, 59]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Devotees offer their hair (Mundan/Kalyanakatta) and make offerings in the sacred Hundi. [60]
details.Transport_Info: Accessible via the Renigunta or Tirupati East railway stations, followed by a bus journey up the Tirumala hills. [57, 58]

details.Historical_Significance: A highly revered shrine surrounded by Chinar trees. It is uniquely known for its sacred spring, whose water is believed to change color to indicate the future fate of the region.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Devotees predominantly offer milk and kheer (rice pudding) into the sacred spring.
details.Transport_Info: Easily accessible via motor bus or taxi from Srinagar, which is located about 14 miles away.

details.Build_Date: 19th Century (Golden dome added by Maharaja Ranjit Singh)
details.Historical_Significance: One of the 51 Maha Shakti Peethas where Sati's tongue is believed to have fallen. The temple has no idol; instead, natural eternal flames burning seamlessly from the rock crevices are worshipped.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Aarti is performed to the eternal flames. Offerings include rabri (sweet milk) and misri.
details.Transport_Info: Located 30 miles from Kangra. Accessible by road from Pathankot or the Jwalamukhi Road railway station.

details.Build_Date: Rebuilt in the 18th Century by Marathas
details.Historical_Significance: An ancient temple where the Pandavas, along with Lord Krishna, prayed to Lord Shiva before the Mahabharata war and received his blessings for victory. A few drops from its sacred sarovar reportedly cured King Vena's leprosy.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing in the holy Sarovar followed by Jalabhishek of the ancient Shivalinga.
details.Transport_Info: Easily accessible via Thanesar City Railway Station and local road transport.

details.Historical_Significance: Believed to be the incarnation of Yoga Maya who escaped Kansa and established herself in the Vindhya mountains. Forms the focal point of the famous 'Trikon Parikrama'.
details.Festivals_Celebrated: Chaitra Navratri, Ashwin Navratri, Kajali Mahotsav
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Pilgrims perform the Trikon Parikrama covering Vindhyavasini, Ashtabhuja, and Kali Khoh shrines.
details.Transport_Info: Located 5 miles from Mirzapur. Vindhyachal is a prominent railway station on the Delhi-Howrah route.

details.Historical_Significance: One of the 12 Jyotirlingas and also a Shakti Peetha where Sati's heart fell. The Jyotirlinga was brought by Ravana from Kailash but got permanently rooted here.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Millions of 'Kanwariyas' fetch holy water from Sultanganj and trek to pour it over the Jyotirlinga during the month of Shravan.
details.Transport_Info: Located just 4 miles from Jasidih railway station on the Howrah-Patna main line.

details.Historical_Significance: Founded by the great Vaishnavite saint Shri Madhvacharya. The temple is unique globally because the Lord is worshipped only through a silver-plated window with nine holes, known as the Navagraha Kindi.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Elaborate daily offerings and chanting of Dwaita philosophy scriptures. Darshan is strictly through the Navagraha window.
details.Transport_Info: Located 37 miles from Mangalore; very well connected by the Konkan Railway and state buses.

details.Historical_Significance: Commissioned by the Hoysala King Vishnuvardhana. It is globally acclaimed for its exceptionally intricate soapstone carvings, star-shaped layout, and beautiful Madanika (celestial nymph) sculptures.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Daily Agamic poojas dedicated to Lord Vishnu and his consorts.
details.Transport_Info: Located about 24 miles from Hassan Railway Station. Well connected by road from Bengaluru and Mysore.

details.Historical_Significance: A major Shakti Peetha where Goddess Sati's navel fell. It is famously known as Nabhi Gaya, making it an extremely prominent and holy site for performing ancestral rites (Shraddha).
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Pilgrims perform Pind Daan (ancestral rites) at the Nabhi Gaya well and bathe in the Vaitarani river.
details.Transport_Info: Located about 9 miles from the Jajpur Keonjhar Road railway station on the Howrah-Waltair line.

details.Build_Date: 10th - 14th Centuries (Expanded heavily by Chola and Pandya kings)
details.Historical_Significance: Foremost of the 108 Divya Desams (Swayam Vyakta Kshetra). Enclosed by seven concentric walls, it is the largest functioning Hindu temple complex in the world. Sri Ramanujacharya lived and taught here.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Daily worship follows the strict Pancharatra Agama. Devotees walk through the paramapada vaasal (heaven's gate) during Ekadashi.
details.Transport_Info: Srirangam is situated on an island just across the Cauvery river from Tiruchirappalli (Trichy), which has a major railway junction and airport.

details.Historical_Significance: Represents the Akasha (Aether/Space) element among the Pancha Bhoota Sthalas. It is famous for the 'Chidambara Rahasyam' (secret of Chidambaram) and is one of the rare temples where both Shiva and Vishnu are enshrined in close proximity.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Witnessing the Maha Aarti of the dancing Lord Nataraja and viewing the empty space symbolizing the Akasha Lingam.
details.Transport_Info: Chidambaram has its own railway station located on the main line between Chennai and Tiruchirappalli.

details.Historical_Significance: According to legend, Lord Krishna traveled here from Dwarka to honor the deep devotion of His aged devotee, Bodana. The temple houses the original idol brought from Dwarka.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Daily Mangala Aarti, Rajbhog, and Shringara Darshan.
details.Transport_Info: Dakor is easily accessible by rail on the Anand-Godhra line and by state buses from Ahmedabad and Vadodara.

details.Historical_Significance: One of the 12 revered Jyotirlingas, situated in the ancient Dakini forest on the Sahyadri mountains. It is the origin of the Bhima river. Legend states Lord Shiva rested here after slaying the demon Tripurasura.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Rudrabhishek and Panchamrit Pooja performed daily.
details.Transport_Info: Accessible via a scenic, hilly road roughly 68 miles (110 km) from Pune.

details.Historical_Significance: The 12th Jyotirlinga, located extremely close to the famous Ellora Caves. The legend involves Lord Shiva resurrecting the dead son of his devout worshipper, Ghushma, and manifesting here eternally at her request.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Daily Jalabhishek and Shringar Aarti.
details.Transport_Info: Located about 12 miles from Daulatabad and 18 miles from Aurangabad city. Well connected by road.

details.Historical_Significance: Houses the sacred 'Atmalinga' brought from Kailash by Ravana. Lord Ganesha deceitfully placed it on the ground, making it permanently rooted. It is revered on par with Kashi.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Darshan of the Atmalinga (only visible completely during the Ashtabandhana festival occurring once every 20 years).
details.Transport_Info: Gokarna Road railway station is on the Konkan Railway network, easily accessible by buses from Mangalore or Goa.

details.Historical_Significance: Believed to be the exact location where Lord Narasimha emerged from the pillar to slay Hiranyakashipu and save Prahlada. The surrounding hills house nine distinct shrines known as Nava Narasimha.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Thirumanjanam and specific Agamic daily Aradhanas.
details.Transport_Info: Accessible by road from Nandyal or Cuddapah; involves trekking to visit all nine hill shrines.

details.Historical_Significance: Legend says Lord Vishnu manifested here to save Prahlada from being thrown into the sea under a mountain. Uniquely, the deity is covered completely in sandalwood paste year-round, appearing like a Shiva Linga.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Nijaroopa Darshan (true form without sandalwood paste) is allowed only for 12 hours once a year on Akshaya Tritiya.
details.Transport_Info: Located on a hill about 10 miles from Visakhapatnam railway station, accessible by local buses and taxis.

details.Historical_Significance: An ancient Vaishnavite pilgrimage center on the banks of the Meshwo river. The temple walls contain exquisite stone carvings depicting scenes from the Ramayana and Mahabharata.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing in the holy Meshwo river (Nagadhara) before the daily Aarti.
details.Transport_Info: Situated on the Delhi-Mumbai National Highway (NH-8), with excellent road connectivity.

details.Historical_Significance: Revered as one of the 51 Shakti Peethas. Goddess Tulja Bhavani is the Kuladevi (family deity) of the Maratha royal family, including Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, whom she is believed to have gifted a magical sword.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Chhakdi Puja and ceremonial Abhishekam of the Goddess.
details.Transport_Info: The nearest major railway station is Solapur, about 28 miles (45 km) away, connected by state transport buses.

details.Historical_Significance: Represents the Vayu (Air) element among the Pancha Bhoota Sthalas. Highly famous for the devotion of Kannappa Nayanar, the hunter who plucked out his own eyes to offer them to the bleeding Lingam.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Rahu-Ketu Sarpadosha Nivarana Pooja. Priests do not touch the main Vayu Linga during worship.
details.Transport_Info: Located on the banks of the Swarnamukhi river, about 22 miles from Tirupati. Srikalahasti has its own railway station.

details.Historical_Significance: Represents the Appu (Water) element. The Shiva Linga constantly emits water from an underground spring. A legendary rivalry between a spider and an elephant worshipping the deity here resulted in both achieving salvation.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: During Uchikala Pooja, the priest dresses in female attire to worship Lord Shiva, honoring Goddess Parvati's penance here.
details.Transport_Info: Located in Srirangam Island, just a short distance across the Cauvery river from Tiruchirappalli Railway Station.

details.Historical_Significance: This marks the exact site of Daksha Prajapati's legendary Yajna. It is the sacred place where Goddess Sati self-immolated after her husband, Lord Shiva, was insulted, leading to the creation of the Shakti Peethas.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Rudrabhishek and offering holy Ganga water to the Shivling.
details.Transport_Info: Located about 2.5 miles (4 km) from Haridwar Railway Station, accessible by auto-rickshaw or tonga.

details.Historical_Significance: The temple is built over the prison cell (Garbha Griha) where Lord Krishna is believed to have been born to Devaki and Vasudeva under the tyrannical rule of King Kansa.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Daily Mangala Aarti, Bhagavat Katha reading, and Chappan Bhog offering.
details.Transport_Info: Easily accessible from Mathura Junction railway station (less than 2 miles away).

details.Historical_Significance: The idol of Banke Bihari was manifested by the great saint and musician Swami Haridas in Nidhivan. It is one of the holiest and most famous temples dedicated to Lord Krishna.
details.Festivals_Celebrated: Janmashtami, Holi (Phoolon Wali Holi), Jhulan Yatra
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Shringar, Rajbhog, and Shayan. Mangala Aarti is performed only once a year on Janmashtami.
details.Transport_Info: Located in Vrindavan, 9 miles from Mathura. Accessible by auto, taxi, and local buses.

details.Deity: Lord Rama, Shiva, and various deities
details.Build_Date: Pre-Islamic Era (Enclosed by Fort in 1583 CE)
details.Historical_Significance: An ancient underground temple housing the immortal banyan tree (Akshayavat). It is believed Lord Rama visited it, and Chinese traveler Xuanzang recorded its existence.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Pind Daan (ancestral rites) and holy dip at the Triveni Sangam nearby.
details.Transport_Info: Located inside the Allahabad Fort near the Sangam, accessible from Prayagraj Junction railway station.

details.Historical_Significance: Believed to be the manifestation site of the girl child (Yogamaya) who escaped from Kansa's hands. It forms an integral part of the famous Vindhyachal Trikon Parikrama.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Devotees perform the Trikon Parikrama, circumambulating Vindhyavasini, Ashtabhuja, and Kali Khoh shrines.
details.Transport_Info: Located atop a hill about 5 miles from Mirzapur. Accessible via Vindhyachal railway station.

details.Build_Date: Rebuilt multiple times (Current modern structure 20th Century)
details.Historical_Significance: The central seat of the Nath Sampradaya. The temple stands on the exact spot where the great ascetic Guru Gorakhnath meditated.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Offering of Khichdi on Makar Sankranti and daily yoga/meditation practices.
details.Transport_Info: Centrally located in Gorakhpur city, very close to Gorakhpur Junction railway station.

details.Historical_Significance: A UNESCO World Heritage Site. It marks the exact location where Prince Siddhartha Gautama attained supreme enlightenment beneath the sacred Bodhi Tree.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Meditation, chanting of Sutras, and circumambulation of the Bodhi Tree.
details.Transport_Info: Located about 10 miles from Gaya Junction railway station; accessible by auto and taxi.

details.Historical_Significance: Considered a highly sacred spot, it marks the exact source of the holy River Narmada and River Son. The complex houses ancient shrines built by Kalachuri kings.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Evening Narmada Aarti and initiation of the Narmada Parikrama.
details.Transport_Info: Pendra Road is the nearest railway station (26 miles away), well connected by buses.

details.Historical_Significance: A UNESCO World Heritage Site built by King Narasimhadeva I. It is designed as a colossal, elaborately carved chariot for the Sun God, with 24 wheels.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Currently a heritage site with limited active worship, but pilgrims bathe at the Chandrabhaga beach.
details.Transport_Info: Located 22 miles (35 km) from Puri via Marine Drive road.

details.Historical_Significance: The largest and most ornate Hindu temple in the medieval temple group at Khajuraho. Famous worldwide for its intricate, exquisite sandstone carvings.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Functions primarily as a monument, but Shivaratri is observed.
details.Transport_Info: Khajuraho has its own airport and railway station, making access very convenient.

details.Historical_Significance: A vast ancient water tank where Lord Brahma is said to have created the universe. Located near Jyotisar, where Lord Krishna delivered the Bhagavad Gita.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Taking a holy dip during a solar eclipse is believed to equal the merit of performing an Ashvamedha Yajna.
details.Transport_Info: Kurukshetra (Thanesar) is well-connected by road and rail on the Delhi-Ambala line.

details.Historical_Significance: One of the 51 revered Maha Shakti Peethas. It is believed that the left breast of Goddess Sati fell here.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: During Makar Sankranti, the idol is covered with a thick layer of butter as a cooling remedy for the wounds Goddess Sati received.
details.Transport_Info: Kangra railway station is on the narrow-gauge line; also accessible by road from Pathankot.

details.Historical_Significance: Situated at the southernmost tip of India where three oceans meet. The virgin goddess performed penance here to marry Lord Shiva. Known for her brilliantly sparkling diamond nose ring.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Holy dip at the Triveni Sangam of the oceans before entering the temple.
details.Transport_Info: Kanyakumari has its own railway terminus, well connected to major cities.

details.Historical_Significance: One of the 51 Shakti Peethas and the center of Kanchipuram. Uniquely, the goddess sits in a majestic Padmasana (yogic posture) instead of a standing pose. Adi Shankaracharya established the Sri Chakra here.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Worship is offered to the Sri Chakra placed in front of the deity.
details.Transport_Info: Kanchipuram is well-connected by road and rail, about 45 miles from Chennai.

details.Historical_Significance: One of the 51 supreme Maha Shakti Peethas. It is believed that the right toes of Goddess Sati fell at this spot. The temple is situated on the banks of the Adi Ganga.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Daily Kali Puja, offering of red hibiscus flowers, and traditional animal sacrifice rituals (symbolic).
details.Transport_Info: Easily accessible via Kalighat Metro Station and local buses in South Kolkata.

details.Historical_Significance: Built by the philanthropist Rani Rashmoni. This iconic temple is globally renowned for its association with the great 19th-century mystic, Sri Ramakrishna Paramahamsa, who served as its priest.
details.Festivals_Celebrated: Kali Puja, Kalpataru Utsav, Snana Yatra
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing in the Hooghly river (Ganges) and attending the grand evening Sandhya Aarti.
details.Transport_Info: Well connected via Dakshineswar Railway Station and local ferries across the Hooghly River.

details.Historical_Significance: A major pilgrimage center for the Shaiva sect in eastern India. Built by Raja Bharamalla after he discovered a self-manifested (Swayambhu) Shiva Linga hidden in the forest.
details.Festivals_Celebrated: Maha Shivaratri, Chaitra Sankranti, Shravan Mela
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Kanwar Yatra: Devotees fetch holy water from the Ganges to pour over the Jyotirlinga.
details.Transport_Info: Accessible via Tarakeshwar Railway Station, located about 36 miles (58 km) from Kolkata.

details.Historical_Significance: Marks the sacred confluence where the River Ganges meets the Bay of Bengal. It is the site where King Sagara's 60,000 sons were turned to ashes by Kapil Muni and later liberated by the Ganges.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Taking a holy dip at the confluence (Sangam) specifically on Makar Sankranti to wash away lifelong sins.
details.Transport_Info: Reached by a bus/train to Kakdwip (Harwood Point) and a subsequent ferry ride across the Muriganga river.

details.Deity: Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and Radha Krishna
details.Historical_Significance: Navadvipa (Nabadwip) is the supreme holy land for Gaudiya Vaishnavism, celebrated as the birthplace of Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, considered the combined incarnation of Radha and Krishna.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Performing the Navadvipa Mandala Parikrama across the nine islands and continuous Harinam Sankirtan.
details.Transport_Info: Nabadwip Dham is a major railway station on the Howrah-Bandel-Katwa line.

details.Historical_Significance: A UNESCO World Heritage Site located on the banks of the sacred Bagmati River. It is considered one of the holiest and most significant temples of Lord Shiva in the world.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Darshan of the four-faced Lingam, Bagmati river holy dip, and offering of Rudrabhishek.
details.Transport_Info: Located just 3 miles from Kathmandu city center and near Tribhuvan International Airport.

details.Historical_Significance: Located at an altitude of 12,172 ft in the Himalayas. Revered heavily by both Hindus and Buddhists as a place of liberation (Moksha). It is a premier site for finding sacred Shaligram stones.
details.Managing_Authority: Local Buddhist Nuns and Brahmin Priests
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing in the freezing 108 waterspouts (Mukti Dhara) and viewing the eternal natural flame.
details.Transport_Info: Accessible via flight to Jomsom, followed by a rough jeep ride or trek through the mountains.

details.Historical_Significance: Believed by many scriptures and devotees to be the genuine Vaidyanath Jyotirlinga. The area is rich in medicinal herbs, linking Shiva to 'Vaidya' (the supreme healer).
details.Festivals_Celebrated: Maha Shivaratri, Shravan Somvar, Champa Shashthi
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Devotees are allowed to touch the Lingam and perform Abhishek directly.
details.Transport_Info: Parli Vaijnath is a prominent railway station well connected on the South Central Railway network.

details.Historical_Significance: Considered by many traditions to be the Nageshwar Jyotirlinga mentioned in the Puranas as located in the legendary Darukavana forest. Associated deeply with the Varkari saint Namdev.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: The main Jyotirlinga is situated in an underground garbhagriha, requiring devotees to descend a narrow staircase for darshan.
details.Transport_Info: Located 12 miles from Chondi Railway Station and easily accessible by road from Hingoli and Parbhani.

details.Historical_Significance: Kamadgiri is the forested hill revered as the holy embodiment of Lord Rama. It is the core sacred site of Chitrakoot where Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana spent most of their exile.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Devotees perform the 5-kilometer barefoot Parikrama (circumambulation) of the Kamadgiri hill to fulfill wishes.
details.Transport_Info: Chitrakoot Dham Karwi is the nearest major railway station, well-connected to major Indian cities.

details.Historical_Significance: Established by the fifth Sikh Guru. It boasts the largest holy sarovar (water tank) of all the Sikh shrines and is celebrated for curing leprosy and physical ailments.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing in the massive Sarovar, attending continuous Gurbani Kirtan, and partaking in Langar.
details.Transport_Info: Located just 14 miles (22 km) south of Amritsar, with an easily accessible railway station.

details.Historical_Significance: Known as the spot where Lord Brahma completed the creation of the universe and placed a sacred peg (Brahma Khunti). Also holds the Valmiki Ashram where Goddess Sita lived in exile and birthed Luv and Kush.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Ganga snan at the Brahmavart Ghat and visiting the birthplace of Luv-Kush.
details.Transport_Info: Located about 14 miles (22 km) upstream from Kanpur city, well-connected by road.

details.Historical_Significance: A monumental complex comprising 108 Shiva temples intricately lined along the crescent curve of the Yamuna River. It was the ancestral hometown of former PM Atal Bihari Vajpayee.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Offering Yamuna water to the 108 Shiva Lingas arrayed along the ghats.
details.Transport_Info: Located 43 miles (70 km) from Agra. Shikohabad is a nearby major railway station.

details.Historical_Significance: The sacred site where Lord Vishnu descended as the Varaha (boar) avatar to lift the earth from the cosmic ocean. Famously recognized as the birthplace of the great poet-saint Goswami Tulsidas.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing in the Har-Ki-Pauri kund (which dissolves bones within days) and Asthi Visarjan.
details.Transport_Info: Soron is a railway station just 9 miles from Kasganj junction on the North Eastern Railway.

details.Historical_Significance: One of the Arupadaiveedu (six holy abodes) of Lord Murugan. Uniquely located directly on the shores of the Bay of Bengal, marking the exact spot where he vanquished the demon Surapadman.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Taking a holy dip in the sea followed by bathing in the Nazhi Kinaru (a sacred freshwater well right on the beach).
details.Transport_Info: Tiruchendur is a terminal railway station 38 miles from Tirunelveli, very well connected by buses.

details.Historical_Significance: This temple marks the sacred venue of the celestial marriage of Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati, witnessed by Lord Vishnu. An eternal flame (Akhand Dhuni) has been burning here since the wedding.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Devotees offer wood to the Akhand Dhuni (eternal flame) and take its sacred ashes as a blessing for marital harmony.
details.Transport_Info: Accessible via a 5-mile trek or a rough motorable road from Sonprayag.

details.Historical_Significance: Located at the holy confluence (Sangam) of the Alaknanda and Bhagirathi rivers where the river Ganges is officially formed. Lord Rama is believed to have performed penance here to absolve himself of the sin of killing Ravana.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing at the sacred Sangam and performing ancestral rites (Pind Daan).
details.Transport_Info: Located about 44 miles from Rishikesh, easily accessible by motor bus on the Badrinath highway.

details.Historical_Significance: The spiritual source of the holy river Ganges. King Bhagirath is said to have prayed here on a sacred rock (Bhagirath Shila) to bring the Goddess Ganga down to earth to absolve the sins of his ancestors.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Attending the evening Ganga Aarti, bathing in the icy Bhagirathi river, and offering Gangajal.
details.Transport_Info: Accessible by a scenic motorable road from Uttarkashi (approx. 60 miles).

details.Historical_Significance: The source of the river Yamuna and the seat of Goddess Yamuna. It features sacred thermal springs like Surya Kund where the water is boiling hot despite the freezing surroundings.
details.Festivals_Celebrated: Akshaya Tritiya, Yamuna Jayanti, Yama Dwitiya
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Devotees cook rice and potatoes in the boiling thermal springs to take home as Prasad. Holy dip in the Jamunabai Kund.
details.Transport_Info: Requires a trek of about 3 to 4 miles starting from Janki Chatti.

details.Historical_Significance: Perched atop the Neel Parvat, it is one of the Siddh Peethas. Legend says Goddess Chandi rested here after slaying the demon kings Shumbha and Nishumbha.
details.Festivals_Celebrated: Chaitra Navratri, Ashwin Navratri, Kumbh Mela
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Offering chunri, coconuts, and sweets to the Goddess for the fulfillment of wishes.
details.Transport_Info: Accessible via a 2-mile steep trek from Chandighat or by taking the Udan Khatola (cable car).

details.Historical_Significance: Famous as the worship place of the legendary warriors Alha and Udal. Situated atop the Trikuta Hill, it is revered as a prominent Siddha Peetha.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Climbing the 1,063 stairs for Darshan and tying a sacred thread around the temple premises for wishes.
details.Transport_Info: Maihar is a major railway station on the Katni-Satna line. A ropeway is available to reach the hilltop temple.

details.Historical_Significance: Located deep inside the historic Kalinjar fort, it houses a giant rock-cut Shivalinga. Legend states Lord Shiva rested here after consuming the Halahala poison to cool his throat.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Jalabhishek of the Lingam using water that continuously drips naturally from the cave roof.
details.Transport_Info: Located about 45 miles from Banda and 20 miles from Badausa railway station.

details.Historical_Significance: The sacred place where Lord Mahavira, the 24th Tirthankara of Jainism, attained Nirvana (salvation). The brilliant white marble temple stands beautifully in the middle of a lotus pond.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Worshipping the sacred footprints (Charan Paduka) of Lord Mahavira placed inside the temple.
details.Transport_Info: Located 9 miles from Bihar Sharif on the Bakhtiarpur-Nawada bus route.

details.Historical_Significance: The most supreme Jain pilgrimage site globally. It is believed that 20 out of the 24 Jain Tirthankaras attained Moksha (salvation) from the peaks of these hills.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Devotees undertake a grueling 18-mile barefoot trek covering all the Tonks (shrines) on the mountain peaks.
details.Transport_Info: Located near Parasnath Railway Station on the Howrah-Gaya line. The trek base starts from Madhuban.

details.Historical_Significance: A famous Jain pilgrim center known for its megalithic 84-feet high statue of Lord Adinath carved out of a single rock in the Satpura range.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Climbing the mountain to perform Darshan of the giant statue and partaking in the grand Abhisheka.
details.Transport_Info: Accessible by road from Barwani city, which is approximately 5 miles away.

details.Historical_Significance: Situated on the summit of Pavagadh Hill, it is one of the 51 Shakti Peethas. It is believed that the right toe of Goddess Sati fell here. Sage Vishwamitra is said to have worshipped the Goddess here.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Devotees offer chunri and coconuts to the deity after a steep climb or a ropeway ride.
details.Transport_Info: Champaner Road station is nearby. A modern ropeway is available to ferry pilgrims to the summit.

details.Historical_Significance: An ancient Hindu temple built by the Shilahara king Mahamandaleshwar. It is a masterpiece of stone carving in the Bhumija style, with the sanctum situated uniquely below ground level.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Devotees descend stone steps into the dark sanctum sanctorum to worship the Swayambhu Shivalinga.
details.Transport_Info: Located just 1 mile from Ambarnath Railway Station on the Mumbai-Pune line.

details.Historical_Significance: One of the 18 Maha Shakti Peethas in India. It is believed that the Goddess resides here to protect the region from demons. The idol is made of gemstone and weighs about 40 kg.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Kumkumarchana, Abhishekam, and witnessing the 'Kiran Utsav' where the setting sun's rays directly illuminate the deity.
details.Transport_Info: Easily accessible as it is in the center of Kolhapur city, close to the railway station.

details.Historical_Significance: The first of the four Amnaya Peethams established by Adi Shankaracharya. The Goddess of wisdom and learning is seated here on the banks of the sacred Tunga river.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Performing 'Aksharabhyasam' (initiating children into education) and feeding the sacred fish in the Tunga river.
details.Transport_Info: Best reached by road from Udupi, Mangalore, or Chikkamagaluru.

details.Historical_Significance: A pinnacle of Hoysala architecture, famous globally for its incredibly intricate soapstone carvings detailing Hindu epics. The temple was built by King Vishnuvardhana.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Principally maintained as a heritage monument, but active worship continues for Lord Shiva in the inner sanctum.
details.Transport_Info: Located about 20 miles from Hassan. Buses run regularly from Hassan and Belur.
